applications.name: MK_editor
applications.purpose: Optional desktop editor shell built by the dependency-free `desktop-editor` lane. The previous SDL3/Dear ImGui shell has been replaced in the active `MK_editor` target by a clean Win32 first-party retained shell owned privately under `editor/src`, with `MK_editor_core` remaining the supported editor logic and AI-operable retained-model surface for project IO, authoring, core-backed `EditorDockLayout` / `editor_dock_panel_catalog` dock and panel identity rows, value-only `EditorDockCommandKind` / `EditorDockCommandRequest` / `EditorDockCommandPlan` dock mutation planners, first-party `EditorDockMultiWindowLayout` / `EditorDockWindowCommandPlan` multi-window dock planners, clean-break `GameEngine.Workspace.v3` persistence, core-backed `EditorRichText*` document/selection/copy/view-model/AI/edit rows, `EditorRichTextEditCommandKind`, `EditorRichTextEditRequest`, `EditorRichTextEditResult`, `EditorRichTextClipboardPayload`, `editor_rich_text_revision`, `normalize_editor_rich_text_selection`, `apply_editor_rich_text_edit_command`, editable AI command ids `<rich_text_document_id>.insert_text` through `<rich_text_document_id>.paste_rich_text`, profiler rows, generated game studio rows, input rebinding rows, play-in-editor review rows, game-module driver tests, material preview retained rows, value-only `EditorUiPerformanceBudget` / `EditorUiPerformanceSample` measured baseline budget summaries, first-party retained `RetainedUiSnapshot` / `diff_retained_ui_snapshots` frame-to-frame cache evidence, and the first native panel set. The shell renders Main Menu, Scene, Inspector, Assets, Console, Viewport, Resources, AI Commands, Profiler, Timeline, and Project Settings panels from the core dock layout/catalog stored on `Workspace` through `mirakana::ui` documents and `MK_ui_renderer` submissions, binds private Win32 file-dialog, clipboard, reviewed process-runner, Windows SDK DirectWrite text-layout/glyph-raster service adapters, a private Windows TSF text-input/IME session adapter selected through first-party UI contracts, first-party TextInputParityEvidenceRequest rows, private NativeEditorTsfTextStoreEvidence over app-owned ITextStoreACP, and a private Windows UIA provider tree published from retained accessibility rows with screen-space bounds and UIA runtime-id rules, and `ui_retained_diff_status=ready`, `ui_retained_dirty_rows=0`, positive retained layout/text/submission cache hit rows, zero retained layout/text cache miss rows, `ui_retained_submission_rebuilt_rows=0`, and `ui_retained_cache_native_handle_access=0` smoke counters. `main_menu` is shell chrome in the catalog, while `input_rebinding` remains a workspace panel outside the current native shell panel set. Dock command planning and AI operation overloads can inspect dock layout rows, read rich_text_rows, show, hide, activate, move, split, and reset tab-stack layouts, and plan/apply multi-window commands `editor.dock.window.create`, `editor.dock.window.close`, `editor.dock.panel.tear_off`, `editor.dock.panel.move_to_window`, `editor.dock.window.merge`, and `editor.dock.window.reset_all` through reviewed dry-run/apply rows; clean-break GameEngine.Workspace.v2 persists dock graph rows and clean-break GameEngine.Workspace.v3 persists multi-window dock rows through MK_editor_core text-store IO. The shell now renders single-window dock tab headers, disabled hidden tabs, split gutters, active/focused panel state, keyboard focus traversal over dock tabs, Console diagnostics and AI Commands status/command/evidence rows as read-only rich-text spans, Inspector property rows with visible edit command controls only for editable rich-text documents, and a focused Project Settings text field with private Windows TSF session selection, first-party caret rect, surrounding text, composition, commit, cancel, strict cursor/selection validation, fail-closed adapter dispatch rows, TextInputParityEvidenceRequest evidence, and app-owned ITextStoreACP text-store rows; it validates selected DirectWrite text shaping and glyph rasterization through existing `ITextShapingAdapter` / `IFontRasterizerAdapter` contracts, records selected text atlas handoff evidence rows, records retained UI diff/cache summaries and multi-window dock/workspace v3 rows without requesting native handles, and publishes a private UIA provider from semantic role/name/state/focus/action/relationship/navigation rows with screen-space bounds, a hosted-root null runtime id contract, child `UiaAppendRuntimeId` rows, first-party control pattern/event/live-region evidence rows, and no native handle exposure. Visible OS-level multi-window drag/drop shell restoration, help rich text, Direct2D GPU text rendering/upload, broad shaping/bidi/fallback, custom native IME candidate UI rendering, non-Windows IME execution, external OS accessibility-tool execution beyond first-party UIA pattern/event evidence, cross-platform accessibility parity, actual macOS/Linux visible shell execution, Android/iOS visible editor shells, and cross-platform font adapters remain later private shell phases. `EditorCrossPlatformShellAdapterPlan` now records host-gated first-party macOS AppKit/Core Text/NSTextInputClient/NSAccessibilityProtocol/Metal presentation rows, host-gated Linux X11-or-Wayland selection/AT-SPI2/IBus/Fcitx/Vulkan presentation rows, unsupported Android/iOS visible shell rows, `editor.ai.shell.cross_platform`, and no native handle exposure. The Viewport and Material Preview surfaces now promote to private visible D3D12 texture readiness only after private D3D12 texture adapters and a private visible compositor own requested display gates, offscreen render targets, barriers, descriptor leases, visible panel/composite evidence, material uploads, swapchain presentation, and viewport-only resize-safe teardown inside `editor/src`; host/toolchain-gated Vulkan texture display requires Vulkan host evidence, validation layer, SPIR-V, synchronization2, private compositor sampling, and no native handle exposure before `vulkan_texture_ready`; Apple-host-gated Metal texture display requires the reviewed `renderer-metal-apple-host-evidence` recipe, command queue, metallib, feature family/set, render pipeline/pass, render-target texture, shader-read sampling, sampler state, drawable present, command-buffer completion, private compositor sampling, and no native handle exposure before `metal_texture_ready`. Default visible-shell Vulkan/Metal backend selection and broader material-preview GPU parity remain unsupported until focused backend phases validate them. `tools/build-editor.ps1` configures, builds, and tests the current native editor lane, and `tools/evaluate-cpp23.ps1 -Editor` covers the current C++23 editor preset. The shell must not depend on SDL3, Dear ImGui, Qt, Slint, RmlUi, or UI middleware, and must not expose Win32, D3D12, DXGI, DirectWrite, TSF, UIA, COM, swapchain, descriptor heap, clipboard, process, file-dialog, texture, or UI-middleware handles through public editor, engine, gameplay, or runtime UI APIs.
